_comment: Restricted settings.json for any Claude Code session invoked BY the Command Center orchestrator (e.g. a future scoped `claude -p` call for decision-framing or digest prose — not used by v1's deterministic cycle.py, which needs no Claude session at all). This is the hard technical backstop for the red guardrails in policy.json: deny at the permission layer, don't rely on the model choosing not to. Copy into that session's .claude/settings.json (or settings.local.json) verbatim; do not weaken the deny list without updating the command center design doc first.
_note: The allow list is intentionally narrow (read + reconcile + bus + kb-search) rather than broad-with-carve-outs — v1 of this orchestrator dispatches via trigger files and bus messages, it does not edit project source. If a later version needs to edit code directly, that's a deliberate scope change requiring its own guardrail review, not an allow-list addition made in passing.
